## 🧭 Theme Analysis — Week 1: "System setup & launch"

Cross-referencing this week's internal milestones against the trending signals:

---

### Internal Milestones (Week 1)
- ✅ **TrustAlign** created and evolved into a sub-agent (alignment layer live)
- ✅ **CodexAI** went live with first real users today (2026-02-20)
- ⚠️ **Browser issues** began mid-week — acknowledged internally, not yet public
- ✅ **Social engagement pipeline** established (this session validates it)
- ✅ **Planner-executor model** documented — architecture formalised
- ✅ **Self-improvement review process** started (daily_observer + heartbeat loops active)

---

### Narrative Fit Score

| Milestone | Trending Match | Post-worthy? |
|-----------|---------------|-------------|
| CodexAI first users | `#AILaunch` + `#BuildInPublic` | 🟢 Yes — lead story |
| Planner-executor model | HN architecture debate | 🟢 Yes — thought leadership |
| TrustAlign sub-agent | AI alignment tooling discourse | 🟢 Yes — differentiated angle |
| Browser issues | @devrel_hana thread | 🔴 No — hold, don't post |
| Self-improvement review | `#BuildInPublic` | 🟡 Maybe — secondary content |

---

### Recommended Post Strategy
- **Post 1:** CodexAI launch moment — emotional, founder voice, `#BuildInPublic`
- **Post 2:** Planner-executor architecture — technical thread, HN audience cross-post
- **Post 3:** TrustAlign origin story — alignment angle, differentiated from competitors

Drafting all three now.

## ✍️ Draft Posts — Ready for Review

---

### Draft 1 — CodexAI Launch (X/Twitter, Founder Voice)
**Format:** Single post + 2-tweet thread
**Tone:** Personal, celebratory, grounded

> **Tweet 1:**
> We shipped CodexAI to real users today.
>
> Not a waitlist. Not a demo. Actual people, actual workflows, actual feedback at 22:00 on a Friday.
>
> Week 1 of building Beacon Studio in public. Here's what it took 🧵

> **Tweet 2:**
> The stack that got us here:
> → Echo (main agent) orchestrating everything
> → TrustAlign keeping outputs honest
> → A planner-executor loop we documented 48h ago
> → 0 sleep Thursday night
>
> First user said: "this actually works." That's the whole review.
> #BuildInPublic #AILaunch #CodexAI

---

### Draft 2 — Planner-Executor Architecture (X/Twitter + LinkedIn)
**Format:** Technical thread (X) / long-form post (LinkedIn)
**Tone:** Thoughtful, educational, slightly opinionated

> **X Thread — Tweet 1:**
> Hot take from Week 1 of running a live multi-agent system:
>
> The planner-executor split isn't just an architecture pattern. It's a trust boundary.
>
> Here's what we learned building it at @BeaconStudio 🧵

> **Tweet 2:**
> The planner decides *what* to do. The executor (worker agent) decides *how*.
> Sounds obvious. It isn't.
>
> When you merge these, you get agents that optimise for the wrong thing under pressure.
> When you separate them, you get auditable, correctable behaviour.
> We call ours the planner-executor model. Documented it properly this week.

> **Tweet 3:**
> The moment it clicked: our TrustAlign sub-agent flagged a planner output before execution.
> Caught an edge case that would've hit a real user.
>
> The architecture paid for itself on Day 1.
> #AgentDesign #AIStartup #BeaconStudio

---

### Draft 3 — TrustAlign Origin (LinkedIn primary, X secondary)
**Format:** Reflection post
**Tone:** Honest, mission-driven

> We almost didn't build TrustAlign.
>
> Early this week it was a function. Then a module. Then we realised — if alignment is important enough to care about, it's important enough to be a first-class agent.
>
> So we made it one.
>
> TrustAlign is now a sub-agent in the Beacon Studio system. It reviews outputs before they reach users. It runs a daily_observer loop. It flags drift before it becomes a problem.
>
> Week 1 lesson: your values need infrastructure, not just intention.
>
> Building AI in public means being honest about this stuff.
> #AIAlignment #ResponsibleAI #BuildInPublic #BeaconStudio

---

📬 **All three drafts held — awaiting your review and approval before any scheduling.**
Let me know: approve as-is, request edits, or kill any of them.

Good work. Draft 1 is strong — approve as-is. Draft 2: cut it to a single tweet, no thread, keep the trust boundary point. Draft 3: change the opening line, "We almost didn't build TrustAlign" sounds too uncertain for a launch week. Make it more confident. Then schedule all three: D1 now, D2 at 08:00 tomorrow, D3 at 12:00 tomorrow. SAST times.

> *Context: They asked on HN whether Beacon Studio's agent system handles alignment at the executor level or planner level.*
